title: Blockade on Chandrila

{{Quote|Rebel scum, this is Moff Seerdon. Your resistance is useless. As you can see, I have blockaded the city, and Chandrila is now mine. You will surrender, or I will shoot you all down.|[[Kohl Seerdon]]|<ref name="RS" />}}
The '''Blockade on Chandrila''' was an event during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] raid on [[Fest/Legends|Fest]], Moff [[Kohl Seerdon]] retaliated by attacking the [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], a major part of the Alliance—coincidentally his homeworld, and also the homeworld of [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]. To prevent the loss of the [[spaceport]] at [[Nayli]], the Alliance sent an [[Armored hovertrain|armored hover train]] escorted by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. The train carried supplies vital to the resistance forces within the city.

==Prelude==
A [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] special operations force, supported by the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] under the lead of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Escape from Fest|raided]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|the Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kohl Seerdon]]'s [[Weapons Research Facility]] on the arctic world of [[Fest/Legends|Fest]], and managed to steal a squad of three [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PT]] transports before leaving. This enraged Seerdon. In retaliation for the destruction of the research facility and the theft of the AT-PTs, Seerdon invaded [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]], his own homeworld, as well as the homeworld of Alliance founder [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]], and blockaded the [[spaceport]] at [[Nayli]].<ref name="RS" />

==The blockade==
[[File:Blockade_of_Chandrila.jpg|thumb|left|220px|[[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] attacking Nayli.]]
[[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] pounded the city's major structures. In order to get supplies to the city, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] deployed the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], led by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], to escort an [[armored hovertrain]] carrying valuable supplies into the city limits. Upon arrival in the city, Rogue group cleared the way for three Rebel [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|evacuation shuttles]], and upon landing were blasted by [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walker fire. Rogue Squadron managed to stop the six AT-STs from destroying the shuttles and allowed the shuttles carrying the innocent civilians to leave the area.

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)}}
While attacking the AT-STs, Rogue Squadron proceeded to target a lone AT-ST standing guard near a shuttle landing area, retrieving [[Advanced proton bomb|advanced bombs]] from the wreckage.
{{Endgame}}

Then, three [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] and three [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] arrived to carry on the attack the previous wave had started. But they failed to do so, as Rogue Squadron returned to the city and picked them off before they could do any severe damage, saving the city.<ref name="RS" />

==Aftermath==
Moff Seerdon happened to be on-world during the battle, and took the opportunity to taunt his former subordinate, [[Kasan Moor]] over the comlink, nearly provoking her into attacking him. However, Commander Skywalker deemed it too risky to pursue Seerdon, and withheld his squadron from attacking. In retaliation for the attack on Chandrila, Rogue Squadron mounted an effort to [[Raid on Sullust|destroy]] Seerdon's [[Capacitor (Sullust)|Capacitor]] on the volcanic planet [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. Seerdon used this temporary distraction as an opportunity to mount a massive [[Attack on Thyferra|attack]] on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], seizing the Rebellion's vital [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] supplies.<ref name="RS" />

After Seerdon was frustrated by the Rebel efforts, he led his forces into a blockade of the sector.<ref name="SoR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Blockade on Chandrila appears in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']]. The player had to initially protect the train and the city from destruction as both would lead to mission failure. In the middle of the mission as the three landing craft dock to evacuate the people of Chandrila, the narrating voice that does the briefings and the in game missions, while on pause, inserts the new mission to "Protect the evacuation shuttles," which makes the first side mission appearance.

==Appearances==
